title: Bharali Namghar
tagline: Bharali Namghar — spiritual town of Assam

overview.short: Bharali Namghar (Assamese: ভঁৰালী নামঘৰ) is a temple situated in Bharali village, India.
overview.description: Bharali Namghar (Assamese: ভঁৰালী নামঘৰ) is a temple situated in Bharali village, India. Bharali is approximately 2 km away from Hatbor, a place of historical importance under the Kaliabor subdivision in the Nagaon district, Assam in India. People from the Nagaon district are mostly agriculture oriented and are guided by rich Sattria (Eksarana religion) culture.

topPlaces.name: Silghat
topPlaces.description: Silghat is a town located on the southern banks of the Brahmaputra, in Nagaon district in the Indian state of Assam. It is 48 km northeast of Nagaon.

items.note: Silghat is a town located on the southern banks of the Brahmaputra, in Nagaon district in the Indian state of Assam. It is 48 km northeast of Nagaon.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Bharali Namghar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Bharali Namghar is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Bharali Namghar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Dimapur Airport (109 km) and the nearest railway station is Dimapur Railway Station (104 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Bharali Namghar?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Bharali Namghar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Bharali Namghar?
faq.a: Stays in Bharali Namghar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
